fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Minnesota Audiologist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a audiologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Minnesota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: audiologist license reciprocity minnesota, how to transfer audiologist license to minnesota, audiologist compact minnesota, minnesota audiologist endorsement application

board.name: Speech-Language Pathologist, Audiologist, and Hearing Instrument Dispenser Advisory Council
board.physical_address: 85 E. 7th Place, Suite 220, St. Paul, MN 55164

license_types.title: AuD, CCC-A
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a audiologist in Minnesota.

steps.title: Complete Doctoral Degree
steps.description: You must earn a Doctor of Audiology (AuD) degree from a program accredited by the Council on Academic Accreditation in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology (CAA) or its equivalent.

steps.title: Pass the Audiology Examination
steps.description: You must pass the audiology examination, administered by a nationally recognized testing organization.

steps.title: Apply for Licensure
steps.description: Submit a completed application to the Minnesota Department of Health, including all required documentation and fees.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the Praxis. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Minnesota audiologist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the Praxis Exam in Audiology is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Minnesota have reciprocity for audiologists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers audiologist practice in Minnesota. To work in Minnesota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my audiologist license to Minnesota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Minnesota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Audiologists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Audiologist in Minnesota
hero.dek: Minnesota Audiologist license requires a doctoral degree in audiology, passing the audiology examination, and completing the application process.
